integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with author: operator, status: pending, and approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-202'
summary: EXP-A1 of the Phase A design refresh is drafted: design token foundation — adds --serif/--sans/--mono plus --ink/--ink-2/--ink-3/--rule/--semantic-accent/--semantic-warn/--semantic-crit/--semantic-good tokens (with dark-theme overrides and print mirrors), and a Google Fonts <head> import for Source Serif 4 + Inter + JetBrains Mono. Purely additive — no existing selector consumes the new tokens yet, body font-family is unchanged. HARD HOLD: do NOT integrate; wait for operator-posted approval HNOTE naming EXP-202. Seven further Phase A EXPs (A2–A8) remain to be drafted in subsequent analyst runs, one per run per Mode 1 policy.
curmudgeon_review_note: This EXP is eligible for adversarial review once drafted. Curmudgeon focus areas: (1) Is Google Fonts the right default given the current zero-third-party posture? (2) Is --semantic- prefix churn worth it vs. overwriting --accent directly? (3) Are the dark-theme token values (warm-grey ink on deep navy) sufficiently distinct from the existing palette to serve the three-role typography system? (4) Is --ink-3 justified, or speculative bloat? Draft must stay DRAFT_AWAITING_HUMAN_GROUP_REVIEW regardless of curmudgeon outcome — the operator-group review is the gate, not the curmudgeon.
